channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In South Dakota, public media endures a year after federal funding was wiped out
item.description: We check in on one state public broadcasting network a year after President Trump signed a law ending federal funding of public media.
item.content:encoded: We check in on one state public broadcasting network a year after President Trump signed a law ending federal funding of public media.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Israel's UN envoy on the U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal and the widening Iran war
item.description: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Israel's U.N. ambassador, Danny Danon, about a proposed U.S.-Saudi civilian nuclear deal, the Iran war and Israel's evolving relationship with the U.S.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ailsa Chang talks with Israel's U.N. ambassador, Danny Danon, about a proposed U.S.-Saudi civilian nuclear deal, the Iran war and Israel's evolving relationship with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Aslam

item.title: How a Northwest tribe and an endangered sea creature are helping each other
item.description: Scientists are starting to reintroduce a 20-legged predator to kelp beds off Washington state after an underwater pandemic nearly wiped it out.
item.content:encoded: Scientists are starting to reintroduce a 20-legged predator to kelp beds off Washington state after an underwater pandemic nearly wiped it out.
item.dc:creator: John Ryan

item.title: Tennessee farmers pivot to other crops as looming federal ban on hemp approaches
item.description: In some states, farmers must adjust to changes in the Farm Bill that closed a loophole they used to grow hemp for use in THC products.
item.content:encoded: In some states, farmers must adjust to changes in the Farm Bill that closed a loophole they used to grow hemp for use in THC products.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Man's account sparks concerns about disability care in ICE detention facilities
item.description: Some lawmakers are questioning conditions at immigration detention centers for people with a disability, after one man's ordeal in Georgia went public.
item.content:encoded: Some lawmakers are questioning conditions at immigration detention centers for people with a disability, after one man's ordeal in Georgia went public.
item.dc:creator: Emily W Pearson

item.title: Why the U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a nuclear agreement
item.description: NPR has confirmed a deal that would allow the U.S. to transfer nuclear technology to Saudi Arabia and allow the country to begin enriching uranium for civilian use.
item.content:encoded: NPR has confirmed a deal that would allow the U.S. to transfer nuclear technology to Saudi Arabia and allow the country to begin enriching uranium for civilian use.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Jarenwattananon

item.title: Plas Johnson, the saxophone behind 'The Pink Panther,' dies at 94
item.description: Plas Johnson, the saxophonist behind "The Pink Panther Theme," has died at 94. We remember the Hollywood session legend whose playing shaped decades of music.
item.content:encoded: Plas Johnson, the saxophonist behind "The Pink Panther Theme," has died at 94. We remember the Hollywood session legend whose playing shaped decades of music.
item.dc:creator: Zephyr Weinreich

item.title: Preliminary results of an independent autopsy of Nolan Wells' body are inconclusive
item.description: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells was inconclusive as to the cause of death of the 18-year old who was left on a Mississippi island by friends on July 4th.
item.content:encoded: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells was inconclusive as to the cause of death of the 18-year old who was left on a Mississippi island by friends on July 4th.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Trump says Iran is desperate to talk. Experts see a different reality
item.description: President Trump says Iran is desperate to negotiate — but experts warn the conflict may be drifting further from diplomacy.
item.content:encoded: President Trump says Iran is desperate to negotiate — but experts warn the conflict may be drifting further from diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: After deadly shootings, police experts question how well ICE officers are vetted
item.description: The ICE officer who shot and killed a man in Maine had a history of domestic abuse and mental health issues. Policing experts say these red flags should have come up in the hiring process.
item.content:encoded: The ICE officer who shot and killed a man in Maine had a history of domestic abuse and mental health issues. Policing experts say these red flags should have come up in the hiring process.
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson
